rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. SEAVIEW INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail Bond Pursuant To Penal Code Section 1305(B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT SEAVIEW INSURANCE CO.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail Bond Pursuant To Penal Code Section 1305(B) Granted. Penal Code sec. 1305(b) explicitly provides that obligations under the bond are released if the clerk fails to mail a copy of the notice of forfeiture to the bail agent at the address shown on the bond. The address for the bail agent shown on the bond is a San Jose address. (See Ex. A attached to the Motion.) The address where the clerk sent the notice of forfeiture is a Carlsbad address. (See Ex. C attached to the Motion.) "Penal Code sections 1305 and 1306 are subject to precise and strict construction." (People v. Nat. Automobile and Casualty Ins. Co. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 277, 287-88.) Plaintiff has not established that Defendant had timely actual notice of the forfeiture. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WHITE et al VS. HANDLERY HOTELS INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions For Discovery Abuse
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs ANWAR SHAIKH, BENAZIR SHAIKH's Motion For Sanctions For Discovery Abuse Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to August 1, 2014 to give moving party ANWAR SHAIKH, BENAZIR SHAIKH the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 24 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has no courtesy copies of the papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT MICHAEL O'REILLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the premises liability cause of action. rulings.Case Title: JAMES MONTOYA VS. UNIVERSITY OF PACIFIC, SCHOOL OF DENTRISTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANTS DAVID GIRALDO, UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC, ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The court sustains the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy without leave to amend. The cause of action is duplicative of the 6th cause of action. The court overrules the demurrer to the 8th cause of action for disability harassment. Personnel decisions can support the claim under Roby v. Mckesson Corp. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 686. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the claim and defendant raises a factual issue. The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action for retaliation. Plaintiff fails to allege protected activity per Rope v. Auto-Chlor System of Washington (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 635. Plaintiff merely alleges that he requested a disability accommodation. The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action for failure to prevent because the retaliation cause of action is currently defective. The motion is denied as to the failure to prevent harassment claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EVON CORPORATION VS. JUSTICE INVESTORS,A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Evon Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents To Defendant Portsmouth Square, Inc. Set One, To Compel Production Of Documetns, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 15: Plaintiff Evon Corporation's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents To Defendant Portsmouth Square, Inc. Set One, To Compel Production Of Documetns, And For Monetary Sanctions The motions to compel is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. If the Parties can work out their differences in letter agreement, the Court shall simply confirm that agreement in an order, setting out a due date for production. Any agreement shall include terms for a privilege log and a protective order. Defendants' request for sanctions is DENIED. Monetary sanctions of $5,679.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Evon Corporation and against Defendants Justice Investors, Portsmouth Square Inc, and Geoffrey M. Palermo to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
